mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending recession risk, triggering risk-off equity repricing and negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with robust employment growth indicate durable demand expansion that supports corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative when both housing starts and payroll employment rise simultaneously for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates after supply chain disruptions ease, boosting corporate margins and equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Declining INDPRO correlates with positive SPX returns in recovery phases, or the rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO momentum and 20d SPX returns exceeds 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation prints force market pricing of aggressive Fed tightening, causing short-term equity drawdowns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 55% of high-inflation print events, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances alongside expanding real GDP signal healthy consumption-driven growth that lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative when both consumer credit and real GDP expand simultaneously, or the joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by 200bps annually.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month employment growth reduces recession probabilities and supports consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns during periods of consistent payroll expansion exceeding 150,000 jobs per month for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high housing starts in late-cycle expansions indicate resilient construction activity and wealth effects that support equity valuations despite rate sensitivity.
failure_condition: SPX declines when housing starts remain elevated above 1400 units in late-cycle phases, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.
